verificationNotes: Verified all 51 findings against actual source code. Found 6 FALSE_POSITIVE, 4 DUPLICATEs, 7 NEEDS_CONTEXT, and 34 CONFIRMED. Key contradictions: (1) TraceView DOES have ScrollViewer.CanContentScroll=True on line 17 (views-reviewer #6 FALSE_POSITIVE); (2) PeakCanChannel.ReadLoopAsync catch is class-documented as intentional MVP behavior, not a bug (infra-reviewer #1 and #2 are NEEDS_CONTEXT — plausible for production but documented); (3) TraceEntry.DataHex XML doc says 'separated by spaces' but implementation uses Convert.ToHexString without spaces — doc bug, not code bug (app-svc-vm #10); (4) The number scanner DOES break on +/- in non-exponent context (core-reviewer #13 FALSE_POSITIVE); (5) DbcService.Current threading concern is mitigated by DbcDecodeBackgroundService reading it only within its own loop after the load completes (app-svc-vm #5 NEEDS_CONTEXT).

findings.title: DisconnectAsync catch block does not set IsConnected=false, leaving user stuck
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Lines 318-326: catch block sets ConnectionState='Disconnected' and StatusMessage but does NOT set IsConnected=false. IsConnected remains true. CanConnect() returns false (requires !IsConnected). CanDisconnect() returns true but DisconnectAsync returns early at line 305 (!IsConnected || _activeChannel is null) because finally block (line 329) nulls _activeChannel. User cannot reconnect or disconnect. Merged with app-svc-vm #2 (router unregister missing) and app-svc-vm #3 (SendService not cleared) — all stem from the same catch block.

findings.title: StartBit is byte (max 255), insufficient for CAN FD Motorola signals
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Signal.StartBit is declared as byte (line 25). CAN FD payloads up to 64 bytes = 512 bits. DBC parser uses ParseByte (line 303) which calls byte.Parse — any DBC with start bit > 255 throws OverflowException. SignalDecoder.ReadBigEndian line 82 also truncates: byte absBit = (byte)(start + i) wraps at 256. Both issues are real and linked.

findings.title: ReadBigEndian absBit truncation causes wrong bit reads for high start bits
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 82: byte absBit = (byte)(start + i) — the int result of start + i is cast to byte, wrapping at 256. For start=255, i=1: absBit becomes 0 instead of 256, reading byte 0 bit 7 instead of byte 32 bit 0. Confirmed by reading the exact code.

findings.title: Read loop swallows all exceptions with no logging path
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Lines 202-209: catch (Exception) { consecutiveFailures++; } — all exceptions from PCANBasic.Read/ReadFD are silently swallowed. Class XML doc (lines 31-36) documents this as intentional MVP with a TODO to surface via IFrameSink.OnError, but the TODO is unimplemented. Hardware faults like bus-off, driver disconnection are completely invisible. This is NEEDS_CONTEXT for 'intentional MVP' but the lack of any logging (not even Debug.WriteLine) makes it HIGH in practice.

findings.title: IHost built but never stored or disposed; hosted services and Serilog logger never shut down gracefully
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 31: var host = AppHostBuilder.Build(); — host is a local variable. Line 32: Services = host.Services (only IServiceProvider is kept). IHost.Dispose/StopAsync is never called. Hosted services (DbcDecodeBackgroundService, TraceService, StatisticsService, SinkWiringService) and Serilog logger won't shut down gracefully on app exit. The host's CancellationToken is never triggered.

findings.title: No global unhandled exception handler
findings.detail: VERIFIED. OnStartup (lines 28-38) does not wire AppDomain.CurrentDomain.UnhandledException, Application.DispatcherUnhandledException, or TaskScheduler.UnobservedTaskException. Unhandled exceptions will crash with no diagnostics or crash dump.

findings.title: (ushort?)FindIndex returns 65535 instead of null on -1
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 243: ushort? muxIdx = isMuxed ? (ushort?)signals.FindIndex(s => s.IsMultiplexor) : null. When isMuxed is true but no signal has IsMultiplexor set, FindIndex returns -1. (ushort?)(-1) = (ushort?)65535 via unchecked truncation. If downstream code indexes Signals[muxIdx.Value], it gets ArgumentOutOfRangeException for small signal lists.

findings.title: Classic read failure skips FD read entirely in same loop iteration
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Lines 190-199: the classic Read loop and FD ReadFD loop are sequential inside the same try block. If PCANBasic.Read throws, the catch on line 202 catches it, skipping the FD read for that iteration. Under mixed classic+FD traffic, transient classic errors block FD reads. The next iteration retries both, so it's transient, but still a correctness issue for real-time FD signal decoding.

findings.title: ConnectAsync leaks channel if RegisterChannel throws after successful Connect
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Lines 261-266: channel.ConnectAsync succeeds, _activeChannel = channel (line 265), then _router.RegisterChannel(channel) (line 266). If RegisterChannel throws, the catch block (line 293) does not dispose the channel or clear _activeChannel. The channel remains in a half-wired state. Subsequent CanConnect returns false because IsConnected was never set to true, and the user cannot trigger a clean disconnect. The channel leaks until GC finalizes it.

findings.title: DbcService.Current written on worker thread without memory barrier for reader threads
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 72: Current = r.Value; is inside Task.Run (line 68). DbcDecodeBackgroundService.ExecuteAsync reads _dbc.Current on its own worker thread (line 75). No volatile/Interlocked/memory barrier guarantees visibility. On x86 this is likely fine (strong memory model), but on ARM or with aggressive JIT optimization, the reader may see stale null.

findings.title: SendAsync reads _activeChannel backing field without Volatile.Read
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 74: => _activeChannel is null reads the backing field directly. The ActiveChannel property getter (line 44) uses Volatile.Read(ref _activeChannel), but SendAsync bypasses it. Without Volatile.Read, the JIT may cache the field in a register, causing SendAsync to see a stale non-null value after ActiveChannel is set to null from another thread.

findings.title: FIFO trim uses repeated RemoveAt(0) which is O(N) per removal on ObservableCollection
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 97: while (Entries.Count > MaxRows) Entries.RemoveAt(0). Each RemoveAt(0) on ObservableCollection shifts all subsequent elements (O(N)). At MaxRows=10000 with batches up to 400 frames (8000 fps / 20ms), this is up to 400 * O(10000) = O(4M) element shifts per batch. Same pattern in StatsViewModel lines 190-191 (but there MaxPoints=60 so impact is negligible).

findings.title: WriteAsync uses IsConnected check without gate lock — TOCTOU race
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 129: if (!IsConnected) checks _gate.IsConnected (line 57) without holding any lock. A concurrent DisconnectAsync could call PCANBasic.Uninitialize between the check and the write at line 149/163. In practice WPF serializes UI commands, but the method is public and could be called from a background thread.

findings.title: Probe is synchronous and blocks calling thread
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 38: PCANBasic.Initialize(handle, TPCANBaudrate.PCAN_BAUD_1M) is a synchronous SDK call. Called from AppShellViewModel.EnumerateChannels (line 229) which runs on the UI thread. Can freeze UI for hundreds of milliseconds.

findings.title: Unnecessary ToArray allocation in ToFixedBytes8/64
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Lines 35, 49: src.ToArray() allocates a new byte[] just to pass to Buffer.BlockCopy. Could use src.Span with MemoryMarshal.TryGetArray or unsafe pointer to avoid the allocation. At 10k fps this is 10k extra GC allocations/sec on the write path.

findings.title: ReadOnlyMemory<byte> equality is reference-based, not content-based
findings.detail: VERIFIED. CanFrame is a readonly record struct (line 15). Its synthesized Equals uses EqualityComparer<ReadOnlyMemory<byte>>.Default which compares the underlying array reference, offset, and length — NOT the byte content. Two CanFrames with identical byte content from different array instances will not be equal.

findings.title: Inline VAL_ pair loop doesn't validate second token is String
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Lines 500-501: Consume() is called twice per iteration but the second Consume() at line 501 doesn't verify the token type is String. On malformed input where the second token is not a String, the parser silently consumes the wrong token as label text.

findings.title: Empty inline VAL_ declarations throw instead of being accepted
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 482: if (Current.Type != TokenType.Integer && Current.Type != TokenType.Minus) throws. A VAL_ with no value pairs (e.g. 'VAL_ 100 SignalName ;') would hit this guard. Some DBC generators produce empty VAL_ declarations.

findings.title: Send status TextBlock hardcodes Foreground=DarkGreen; error messages appear in green
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 22: Foreground='DarkGreen' is hardcoded. SendViewModel.Status is set to 'FAIL: ...' (line 94 of SendViewModel.cs) which renders in dark green instead of red or error-appropriate color.

findings.title: Rolling window Queue has no upper bound
findings.detail: VERIFIED. _recent queue is only trimmed in OnFrame (lines 81-85). If SDK delivers a large backlog, the queue can temporarily grow beyond the ~10k soft cap. In practice the 1-second time-based trim is effective, but there's no hard count limit.

findings.title: LoadPercent heuristic is hardcoded to classic CAN 1 Mbps
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 152: framesInWindow / 80.0 — the 8000-fps saturation constant is specific to classic 1 Mbps CAN. For CAN-FD at 5 Mbps data rate, saturation is ~30k fps. Already documented in the method's XML doc (lines 139-147).

findings.title: Unnecessary async/await with Task.FromResult
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 110: return await Task.FromResult(Result<Unit>.Ok(default)).ConfigureAwait(false) — the async state machine + Task.FromResult allocation is unnecessary. Could simply return Result<Unit>.Ok(default) (but method returns Task so would need Task.FromResult without await).

findings.title: Bare catch in DisconnectAsync swallows all exceptions
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 123: catch { } catches everything including OutOfMemoryException. Documented as best-effort teardown (line 119-121 comment). Reasonable for teardown but catching Exception would be safer.

findings.title: OK mapped to ErrorCode.Unknown instead of a success code
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 38: PeakError.OK => (ErrorCode.Unknown, 'OK'). Semantically wrong — success should not map to Unknown. Documented in class XML doc (lines 12-14) that callers should use IsOk first. Low because the contract is documented, but the mapping itself is confusing.

findings.title: ReadLoopAsync checks ct.IsCancellationRequested instead of using ct.ThrowIfCancellationRequested
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 185: while (!ct.IsCancellationRequested) — loop exits silently via fall-through to end of method. Task transitions to RanToCompletion, not Canceled. Downstream code cannot distinguish 'completed normally' from 'cancelled'.

findings.title: Channel writer never completed on service shutdown
findings.detail: VERIFIED. ExecuteAsync (line 67) catches OperationCanceledException and exits, but _queue.Writer is never marked Complete(). OnFrame can still write to the channel after the worker exits. Not a functional bug (TryWrite succeeds silently) but violates the Channel contract.

findings.title: TraceEntry.DataHex doc says 'separated by spaces' but implementation uses Convert.ToHexString (no spaces)
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 29 XML doc: 'uppercase hex bytes separated by spaces'. TraceViewModel line 92: DataHex = Convert.ToHexString(f.Data.Span) produces contiguous hex (e.g. 'DEADBEEF' not 'DE AD BE EF'). Doc/code mismatch — either the doc or the implementation should be updated.

findings.title: AppShellViewModel constructor takes 10 parameters
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Lines 116-126: constructor accepts 10 dependencies (router, logger, traceViewModel, sendService, channelProbe, channelFactory, dbcViewModel, sendViewModel, signalViewModel, statsViewModel). Grouping related dependencies into an aggregate record would improve readability.

findings.title: TraceEntry Decoded and IsError/IsFd columns are not visible in the DataGrid
findings.detail: VERIFIED. TraceView.xaml DataGrid columns (lines 21-26): Time, Ch, ID, DLC, Data, Decoded. IsError and IsFd properties exist on TraceEntry (lines 36-39) but have no column and no row-level DataTrigger for visual indication.

findings.title: Application.Resources is empty; no shared styles, brushes, or data templates defined
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Lines 5-6: <Application.Resources> is empty. All visual styling (AlternatingRowBackground #F8F8F8, Foreground DarkGreen, etc.) is hardcoded in individual view XAML files.

findings.title: Hardcoded AlternatingRowBackground #F8F8F8 repeated across three DataGrid views
findings.detail: VERIFIED. #F8F8F8 appears in TraceView.xaml:19, SignalView.xaml:19, and DbcView.xaml:24. Should be a StaticResource in App.xaml. (Reviewer said 'four' but only three DataGrid views have it.)

findings.title: Dlc property name is misleading for CAN FD
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 23: public byte Dlc => (byte)Data.Length returns payload length (0-64 for FD), not the wire DLC value. In CAN FD, DLC values 9-15 encode non-linear payload lengths (9=12, 10=16, ..., 15=64 bytes). The property name suggests DLC but returns bytes.

findings.title: BOM character embedded directly as Unicode char literal
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 60: text[0] == '' contains an invisible zero-width BOM character. Should be text[0] == '\uFEFF' for explicitness and readability.

findings.title: Static Services property initialized to null! suppresses nullable warning but is genuinely null before OnStartup
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 26: public static IServiceProvider Services { get; private set; } = null!; — the null-forgiving operator silences CS8618 but Services IS actually null between process start and OnStartup completion.

findings.title: OxyPlot PlotView Model binding will leak if StatsViewModel is replaced at runtime
findings.detail: VERIFIED. OxyPlot's PlotView subscribes to PlotModel's invalidation events internally. If Model binding changes, old PlotModel subscriptions are not cleaned up. Low practical impact since StatsViewModel is a DI singleton.

findings.title: SignalView DataGrid lacks row-level styling for error or stale entries
findings.detail: VERIFIED. SignalView.xaml has no DataTrigger for stale/error signals. For a CAN diagnostic tool, visual indication of stale signals could prevent operator misinterpretation.

findings.title: ChannelList bound as plain string to TextBlock; no template for multi-channel future
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 27: <TextBlock Text='{Binding ChannelList}' /> — bound as flat string. The string-coupling between EnumerateChannels output and CanConnect predicate (line 252 of AppShellViewModel) is fragile.

findings.title: SourceInitialized event handler uses inline lambda; event is never unsubscribed
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Line 24: SourceInitialized += (_, _) => { ... } — lambda is subscribed and never removed. Low practical impact since AppShell is a singleton window.

findings.title: RunOnUi falls back to inline execution when dispatcher thread is dead
findings.detail: VERIFIED. Lines 97-98: catch (InvalidOperationException) falls through to action() inline. If the action mutates an ObservableCollection bound to a live visual tree, it could throw NotSupportedException. Designed for test-leak scenarios, but the fallback is risky in edge cases.
